stories.title: Donovan Mitchell Signs $273M Max Extension With Cavaliers

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Donovan Mitchell just bet on himself AND Cleveland.

Spida signed a 4-year, $273M max extension with the Cavs — leaving $80M on the table he could've gotten next year.

Age 29. 7-time All-Star. 27.9 PPG last season. He's IN. #CavsNation

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Donovan Mitchell earns roughly $24,000 per minute played under his new $273M Cavs deal.

That's not a typo.

7 All-Star seasons. Cleveland is his city. Would you wait for more or lock in? #CavsNation

facebook.long_form:
💰 Donovan Mitchell just made one of the biggest loyalty calls in NBA free agency history.

The Cavaliers' star guard signed a 4-year, $273 million max extension on Tuesday — but here's the part that makes it remarkable: Mitchell left approximately $80 million on the table.

He could have waited until next offseason and signed a 5-year deal worth around $353 million. Instead, Spida chose to lock in with Cleveland now. No second-guessing. No flirting with other teams.

At 29 years old, coming off a season where he averaged 27.9 points, 5.7 assists, and 4.5 rebounds in 70 games — his 7th consecutive All-Star campaign — Mitchell made a statement. He wants to win in Cleveland. And he wants to do it soon.

The extension doesn't start until the 2027-28 season (he still earns $50.1M this year). But the message is clear: the Cavs are building something real, and their best player is all in.

With Mitchell locked up long-term, Cleveland is now in a position to make their LeBron pitch — not just with emotion, but with a foundation.

Would YOU have waited for the bigger contract? Tell us below 👇
facebook.image_caption: Spida stays. Mitchell's $273M commitment to Cleveland says everything about where this franchise is headed. 🐊💜🍊 #CavsNation

article.headline: Donovan Mitchell Signs $273M Max Extension With Cavaliers, Leaves $80M on the Table

research.facts:

- Fade Away World — $24,000/minute earnings note
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence — 2+ sources):**
- Mitchell agreed to 4-year, $273M max extension with Cleveland (confirmed July 7)
- Includes player option for 2030-31 and a full trade kicker
- Extension begins 2027-28; earns $50.1M in 2026-27 on existing deal
- Mitchell averaged 27.9 ppg, 5.7 ast, 4.5 reb in 70 games (2025-26 season)
- 7th consecutive All-Star selection
- Mitchell (age 29) left ~$80M on the table vs. waiting for a 5-year ~$353M deal next year
- $24,000/minute earnings calculation circulating on social media
**Story freshness:** July 7 announcement — one day old. Mitchell is the anchor of the Cavaliers' LeBron pursuit narrative.

stories.title: Kyle Lowry Retires as a Toronto Raptor — No. 7 Jersey to Be Retired

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Kyle Lowry signed a one-day contract with Toronto and retired as a Raptor.

No. 7 jersey headed to the rafters. The mayor proclaimed it "Kyle Lowry Day."

"I always said I would retire as a Toronto Raptor."

He meant it. #WeTheNorth

facebook.long_form:
💛❤️ The Toronto Raptors — and all of Canadian basketball — said goodbye to a legend today.

Kyle Lowry signed a ceremonial one-day contract and officially retired as a Toronto Raptor on July 7, 2026. His No. 7 jersey will be raised to the rafters at Scotiabank Arena during the 2026-27 season.

Toronto Mayor Olivia Chow proclaimed July 7 "Kyle Lowry Day" in the city.

At the press conference, Lowry said what his fans always believed he'd say:

"This place has just been special for me since the day I got here. I always said I would retire as a Toronto Raptor."

He meant every word. In 2019, Lowry and the Raptors did the unthinkable — they beat the Golden State Warriors for the franchise's first and only NBA championship. It is the single greatest moment in Canadian sports history, and Lowry was at the center of it.

He spent nine seasons in Toronto (2012-2021), becoming a six-time All-Star and the most beloved Raptor of all time. In retirement, Lowry and his wife Ayahna have become co-owners of the WNBA's Toronto Tempo — staying connected to the city that made him.

Goodbye, No. 7. Thank you for everything. 🏆 #WeTheNorth
facebook.image_caption:
"I always said I would retire as a Toronto Raptor." — Kyle Lowry, July 7, 2026.

No. 7. Going to the rafters. 🏆 #WeTheNorth

article.headline: Kyle Lowry Retires as a Toronto Raptor: 'I Always Said I Would Do This Here'

- Won 2019 NBA Championship with Raptors — verified widely

stories.title: LeBron James Six-Team Shortlist — Sixers Now Live, Decision Pushed to Mid-July

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
LeBron's shortlist is now SIX teams:

Warriors. Cavs. Heat. 76ers. Timberwolves. Nuggets.

The Jaylen Brown trade reportedly piqued his interest in Philly.

Rich Paul: no decision soon — we're looking at mid-July. Which team? #NBAFreeAgency

facebook.long_form:
👑 The wait for LeBron James continues — and the race just got more crowded.

As of July 8, LeBron's agent Rich Paul told Forbes the decision is not coming "anytime soon," and is unlikely in the next few days. We're now looking at mid-July at the earliest. The shortlist has been confirmed by multiple insiders: six teams are legitimately in play.

Warriors. Cavaliers. Heat. 76ers. Timberwolves. Nuggets.

The big new development? The 76ers are now considered a genuine third option. The Jaylen Brown trade reportedly "immediately piqued LeBron's interest" in Philly — imagine him alongside Tyrese Maxey, Brown, and Joel Embiid.

The Warriors and Cavaliers are still being framed as the co-favorites — insider Nick Friedell calls it "50/50 Warriors vs. Cavs." The Warriors have MLE money (~$15M) available after Draymond Green opted out. The Cavs can only offer the veteran minimum (~$3.9M), but they now have Donovan Mitchell locked in on a $273M extension.

The Timberwolves, who added LaMelo Ball this offseason alongside Anthony Edwards, Jaden McDaniels, and Rudy Gobert, are also pitching hard.

At 41, LeBron can pick any situation he wants. The question is: what does he want his final chapter to look like?

Who do you think he's going to? Drop your pick 👇
facebook.image_caption: Six teams. One King. LeBron's free agency decision is coming — but not today. 👑 #NBA

stories.title: Dybantsa vs. Peterson — Summer League's Biggest Matchup Opens Vegas Tonight

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
TONIGHT: No. 1 pick AJ Dybantsa makes his pro debut. No. 2 pick Darryn Peterson comes in off 28 and 25-point games in SLC.

Vegas Summer League. 9 PM ET. ESPN. Thomas & Mack.

Summer League game of the year. #NBA

facebook.long_form:
🏀 The moment NBA fans have been waiting all summer for: tonight, the top two picks in the 2026 NBA Draft face each other for the first time.

AJ Dybantsa (No. 1 overall, Washington Wizards) makes his professional debut at Las Vegas Summer League. Darryn Peterson (No. 2 overall, Utah Jazz) walks in off back-to-back 28-point and 25-point performances at the Salt Lake City Summer League, where the Jazz went 3-0.

Game: Wednesday, July 9 at 9 PM ET on ESPN at the Thomas & Mack Center.

Dybantsa is a 6'9" wing out of BYU who led the entire NCAA in scoring last year — 25.5 points per game, 51% from the field, 77.4% from the free throw line. He's never played a professional game. Tonight changes that.

Peterson, meanwhile, has been electric. He had 28 points, 5 rebounds, and a game-winning overtime performance in his first game — then followed it up with 25 points and 12 assists in Game 2. He sat out Game 3 (rest) and arrives fresh.

NBA.com listed this game as one of the five must-see matchups of the entire Summer League.

Who wins the battle of the top picks tonight? Let us know your prediction 👇 #NBA
facebook.image_caption: No. 1 vs. No. 2. The most anticipated matchup of NBA Summer League 2026 tips off tonight. 🏀 #NBA

article.headline: No. 1 vs. No. 2: Dybantsa and Peterson Open Las Vegas Summer League in the Most Anticipated Rookie Matchup of the Summer

stories.title: Ace Bailey Hip Flexor Scare Clouds Jazz Rookie Trio's Summer League Outlook

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Darryn Peterson won't have backup in Vegas tonight. Ace Bailey (back spasms → hip flexor concern) is OUT for Tuesday per CBS Sports.

Peterson carries the Jazz rookie load solo while Dybantsa is fresh for his pro debut.

High stakes. #NBA #UtahJazz

facebook.long_form:
🚨 The Utah Jazz wanted to debut all three of their prized 2026 rookies in Las Vegas. That plan has hit a wall.

Ace Bailey — one of the Jazz's top prospects from the 2026 NBA Draft class — will NOT play Tuesday per CBS Sports, as a hip flexor concern (originally back spasms from SLC Game 1) continues to sideline him. He missed all three games in Salt Lake City after going down in Game 1 against the Hawks.

The situation is being monitored day-to-day.

That leaves Darryn Peterson as the center of the universe for the Jazz in Vegas. Peterson has been remarkable — 28 points in his debut (with a game-winning overtime play), 25 points and 12 assists in Game 2, rested for Game 3. He's the most talked-about rookie in the country right now.

Third rookie Cody Williams (No. 4 pick) is expected to play and will get more responsibility with Bailey out.

For context, this is still Summer League — these games don't count, and Bailey has plenty of time to heal. But Jazz fans, understandably, are watching closely. The franchise is investing heavily in this rookie class, and seeing one of your key guys go down before Vegas even starts isn't the ideal scenario.

Here's hoping Bailey gets back on the court soon. 🙏 #UtahJazz
facebook.image_caption: Ace Bailey's hip flexor injury puts him on the sidelines for Tuesday's Vegas opener. Darryn Peterson will carry the Jazz load solo. 🙏 #UtahJazz
